parameters.content:
# Asset Catalog QUERY API (WEBHOOK)

Machine-facing JSON API for the asset catalog.
POST /webhook/<uuid> with filters, matching assets returned as JSON.

Auth: x-api-key header (store in your secrets manager)
Data source: asset_catalog (populated by Asset Catalog Datalake Sync)
Pair workflow: Asset Catalog Query Form (same data, HTML output for humans)

Default limit 1000 rows, max 5000. Beyond that, narrow the filter.

Owner: [Owner]

## ENTRY POINT
POST /webhook/asset_catalog-query ,  requires x-api-key header.

Body schema:
{
  "filters": { asset_id, asset_name, admin_group, asset_type, status,
               service_groue, customer, business_unit,
               monitoring_enabled, scanning_enabled, is_critical, is_external },
  "limit": N
}

All filter fields optional. Empty body returns everything up to limit.
POST (not GET) because complex filter objects are cleaner in JSON body.

## DATA SOURCE
Reads all ~88k rows from asset_catalog.
No filtering here ,  filtering happens in JS for flexibility.
Same data source as Query Form.

## FILTER + FORMAT
- Applies contains/equals logic per field (matches Query Form semantics)
- Strips internal fields (id, createdAt, updatedAt) from response
- Enforces 5000-row hard cap
- Returns standardized envelope: {success, query, result, meta}

Contract is stable ,  adding new filter fields is non-breaking.
Renaming or removing existing fields IS breaking for consumers.

## SUCCESS RESPONSE
HTTP 200 with JSON body.
Content-Type: application/json

## ERROR HANDLING
Catches any upstream failure (post-auth).
Returns clean {success: false, error: {...}} with HTTP 500.
Prevents consumers from seeing raw n8n stack traces.

Note: auth failures (bad or missing x-api-key) are handled by n8n's
Header Auth layer directly ,  they never reach this branch.
